transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Jack, we got transmitter and receiver both on, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Low gain antenna is 240 and we're ready for you guys to leave.
Give us a mark on the leave.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Here you go, Jack; we need...The SEP receiver and transmitter (are) both on, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Gene, remember we want a mark when you pass the end of the antenna.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yep, until I get past the end.
I got to get my heading changed about 10 degrees to parallel it.
We're still in the same relative position, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We want to go past the LM at heading 260, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, we want to get at 080 (bearing) and 0.4 (range) and get rid of this charge.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, 17, a couple of words there as you drive along.
Let me give them to you early here.

One, we didn't bother to change all the numbers on the checklist; but, by and large, because we think we're 200 meters east of where we were (planning to land), you should probably increase all those numbers except for the explosive package numbers by about two-tenths to get the distance at which you will come across these areas.
Again it's about 0.4 (or) 0.5 (km) that we expect to deploy EP-4.
The more important number though is that it's 0.2 west of the ALSEP.
As you pass the ALSEP, you might note what the range and distance are reading at that point.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Range is the one that changes on...No, wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Range changes every half...on the half kilometer?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The range is what changes in the middle at 50 meters and 150 meters.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The fender fix is working so far.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get around your flag.
There's your flag way out there, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Jack) Let me get around that.
Man, that's really giving the ALSEP some room.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob; we're still seeing the light-colored gabbroic rocks.
I think the reason I said 50 percent (plagioclase) was because, in this light, they look light-colored, and that's probably largely because of the zap-pit halos.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: In the hand lens, it looked like a standard gabbro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Bob, I'm full out at about 11...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you can turn right, now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm full out at about 11 clicks (11 kilometers per hour) right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you give me a call as you pass by the ALSEP as you get ready to deploy the charge, please?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're almost due south of the ALSEP now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Garbled under Bob) got to work my way through here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Go about 0.2 kilometers further than that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) It's a little rocky out here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: In the area we are now...Did you get a distance?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I want to move over this way just a skosh.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, (we're) just south of my geophone 2 flag now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you just clicked to 4, let's go to 6 then, just past the click on 6.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And they want about (a bearing to the SEP transmitter of) 080.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got to start heading right out here, right toward my topographic...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hole-in-the-Wall should be just to the left of the notch (in the rim of Camelot).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I think we're coming up closer to the rim of Camelot.
It's starting to look like a crater now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Looking down-Sun, I see no major albedo changes except for the very fresh craters which are brighter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; can you swing right out over there...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I'll set it right there in that...Can you move forward, and I'll get it in that little depression.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You see on the other side of the rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Pin 1 is pulled and safe; Pin 2 is pulled and safe; Pin 3.
Ever stop and ask yourself what I'm doing (pulling pins on an explosives charge)!?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...If you can give us a frame count, we'd appreciate it.
And I might remind you two to both check that...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...you're at Min cooling since you've got a long drive ahead of you there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, I lost my sample thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Asking about the charge placement) That look good?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If not, I'll do a partial for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We got to do a partial.
I'd like to know where that sampler is.
Well, we can do without it, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, it's sure be nice to...What did it do; come off the end?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I think I can check it though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) If you go around to seeing that big block there by the ALSEP, then you can forget it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll just come on around, and I'll pick up my tracks.
Do you want to get that sampler?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, one stop here for about 2 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you put it on real quick and...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know why...It was hard to put on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Here, let me hold the end.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Still retracted; let me know when.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No...Let go, let go...No, it's just not hooking.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Try it...Push it in once more.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll just lock...I'll twist it down on there and maybe it'll hold.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll just have to be careful.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You'll have to put it in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Every time you pick your seatbelt up...Here it is, it's untwisted now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're still seeing you in Intermediate.
You probably will want to go to Min before you get back on.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: One other thing I might mention to you guys as you're driving here, Jack, before you start talking again, is that, as you go by Camelot, you might keep an eye out for blocks along the rim there, because, remember, we may be wanting to come back and move Station 5 to an area where there's blocks, unless there are blocks at the present, nominal Station 5.
So you might keep an eye for that and plan for the way back.

A second thing, a reminder: if you do stop for a Rover sample or one thing or another along the way, give us a call and keep us informed, because we're timing you on the way out and the assumption is, of course, that driving time out equals drive-back time.
And we're under a 63-minute limit to get you from the LM out to the Station 2 because of OPS drive-back.
So, keep us informed so we can keep a good tab.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I got the thing "two-blocked", and I'm averaging probably 10 to 11 clicks.
It's not exactly straight-line navigation, but I think I can hold most of it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I tell you, when Gene decides to turn...Whoo!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, a reminder on photos.
Yesterday, you apparently took quite a few on the way back from Station 1 to the SEP, and we're right nominal on budget now.

But, considering the fact that we didn't do much sampling, if you continue to use them at the rate you did yesterday coming back from Station 1, at least as we understand it, you'll be pushing us pretty hard in the (film) budget.
Should be every 50 meters or every 100 meters.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And you want to hear something?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The surface is not changing in terms of the detail.
The surface texture of the fine-grained regolith still has the raindrop pattern.
The blocks still look very much like what we sampled yesterday around the LM.
They're light-colored, apparently gabbros, with zap pits...(correcting himself) zap halos.
Occasional craters show lighter colored ejecta; (that is, craters) all the way down to, say, half a meter in size.
Other craters that are just as blocky as those with bright halos have no brightness associated with them.
Most of the brightest craters have a little central pit in the bottom which is glass lined.
The pit is, maybe, a fifth of the diameter of the crater itself.
It's a fairly standard thing for most of these fresher craters, is that little central pit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we're just south of the (east) rim of Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There is a light mantle on the other side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've got the...Ooh, and there's Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Take a couple of pictures looking at that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, how about a bearing and range there to help us pick out the LM, also.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...We won't have any problem finding blocks on the rim of Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about bearing and range to help us pick out the LM location.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Now that little crater in the ejecta did not...(in the ejecta) of Camelot - at least the rim of Camelot - did not bring up blocks on the rim.
It may have been (an impact in) an old depression.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, there is (an) extremely blocky area.
I think (the planned) Station 5 (location) was over there where that block area is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The light-colored areas on the (overhead) photos are essentially blocky.
Many of them are in the 2- to 3- to 4-meter size range.
All of them look light-colored (and, from a distance), look like the gabbro we sampled.
They have light-halo zap pits on them.
I see only occasional grayer varieties, which I believe are the nonvesicular ones like we also sampled.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But the light-colored gabbros are dominant.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Station 5 would have been - rather than in a light-colored area - would have been in a very blocky area.
Station 5 is probably still very good for blocks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There is probably as big blocks there as anywhere on the rim that we've seen.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We ought to be going between Horatio and Camelot now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to give them a call when we're due south of Camelot and see if they can't get a position on us.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can go around that one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That slowed the speed up a little bit.
You can unwrinkle your toes now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Watch that block there; it's probably more than 14 inches (the Rover clearance).
And I got a fairly close look at the rock, and it is the vesicular...(It) looks very much like the vesicular clinopyroxene (a magnesium/iron/silicate mineral) gabbro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, the surface of Camelot is mantled - or the rim - is mantled with the same dark-gray material, and it has the same surface texture: a very fine raindrop pattern.
The saturation crater size does not look bigger than a half a meter, if that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm going to give you 081, 1.6, and 1.4.
We're south of the center of Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: One crater...(Stops to listen to Bob) Okay.
We ought to see Horatio here pretty quick.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think it's right up in front of us.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
